item.title: Microsoft’s Marquee African Data Center Hits Political Stalemate
item.description: The US tech firm, UAE partner G42 and the Kenyan government appear at an impasse

item.title: Why Scott Bessent Is Stopping in Japan En Route to China
item.description: The Treasury Secretary has a deep knowledge of Japan and its markets

item.title: Starmer Opens Path Toward Full Nationalization of British Steel
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged a step toward full nationalization of British Steel during a crucial speech on Monday designed to save his premiership.

item.title: Circle’s First-Quarter Revenue Increases 20%, Net Income Drops
item.description: Circle Internet Group Inc.’s first-quarter revenue increased 20% while net income declined, as ongoing volatility in cryptocurrency markets roiled earnings from companies across the industry at the start of the year.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Peters Says Inflation Risks Lurk Beneath Stock Peaks
item.description: Global fragmentation, artificial intelligence, and inflation are three defining forces shaping markets which have gained urgency due to recent events such as the war in the Middle East, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Grace Peters.

item.title: Bank of America Tightened Block Trade Rules After Esprit Deal
item.description: Bank of America Corp. adopted new block‑trade guidelines months after a deal now at the center of a criminal trial involving a well-known hedge fund, a Hong Kong court heard Monday.

item.title: Zimbabwe Sovereign Wealth Fund Seeks $250M to Boost Gold Output
item.description: Zimbabwe’s state-owned sovereign wealth fund, Mutapa Investment Fund Ltd., needs $250 million to expand its gold mining operations across the country.

item.title: Rheinmetall and Deutsche Telekom Partner on German Drone Shield
item.description: Germany’s largest defense company and its biggest mobile phone carrier plan to jointly develop a defense shield against drones and acts of sabotage to protect critical infrastructure in their home country.

item.title: Discounts on Russian Oil Widened for First Time Since Iran War
item.description: Discounts on Russia’s flagship crude widened for the first time since the start of the Iran war, as shifting expectations over a possible end to the Middle East conflict rattled oil markets.

item.title: Behind the Claude Frenzy That Ate Up All the Mac Minis
item.description: Apple’s entry-level desktop has become a vital piece of equipment for people using tools like OpenClaw to roll their own AIs for professional and personal tasks.

item.title: Dream Finders Homes Is Said to Bid $704 Million for Rival Beazer
item.description: Dream Finders Homes Inc. is close to announcing a $704 million offer to acquire rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: South Africa Central Bank Names Makrelov as Sixth MPC Member
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank named Konstantin Makrelov as a new member of its interest-rate-setting committee to replace Chris Loewald, who retired in March.

item.title: Investors Cheer as Alphabet Goes From AI Zero to Hero
item.description: The Google parent is challenging Nvidia for the title of world’s largest company.

item.title: Sell Any Rally in Luxury Stocks as Growth Slows, Berenberg Says
item.description: Investors should sell any rally in luxury stocks as the sector grapples with entrenched growth challenges, according to Berenberg analysts.

item.title: Breitling Cuts Jobs as Swiss Franc and Luxury Slump Hit Results
item.description: Breitling AG is laying off dozens of employees as the Swiss watchmaker, whose models include the pilot-focused Navitimer, grapples with softening luxury demand and higher costs.

item.title: India State Refiners Eye Modest Fuel Price Hike as Losses Mount
item.description: Indian state refiners are expecting a modest increase in retail fuel prices within days, as the government seeks to balance the impact of the ten-week war in the Persian Gulf and the needs of consumers.

item.title: Lucrative Lira Bets Are at Risk of Unraveling as Oil Shock Hits Turkey
item.description: A lucrative bet on the Turkish lira is coming under strain as a surge in oil import bills threatens to accelerate the currency’s slide.

item.title: Barrick Pledges Up to $3 Billion in Buybacks Ahead of IPO
item.description: Barrick Mining Corp.’s board has authorized the repurchase of as much as $3 billion of its shares, as the world’s third-largest gold producer woos investors before spinning off its North American assets later this year.

item.title: UK Fintech Wise Makes US Trading Debut With Dual Listing
item.description: Wise Group Plc is making its trading debut in the US with a Nasdaq listing as the financial technology firm tries to take advantage of a larger stock market and its legions of investors.

item.title: Citi’s Manthey Says US Stock Outperformance Has Further to Run
item.description: The outperformance by US equities driven by a narrow group of giant tech stocks is set to persist, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

item.title: A $400 AI Bet That’s Actually a High-Stakes Wager on the Future of Work
item.description: Two prominent economists differ on how quickly artificial intelligence will reshape the US job market.

item.title: Japan Panel Urges BOJ to Take Cautious Approach to Rate Hikes
item.description: A key panel advising Japan’s government urged the Bank of Japan to consider risks of worsening corporate funding conditions when formulating monetary policy, in a sign that Governor Kazuo Ueda may face pushback if he seeks to raise the benchmark rate next month.

item.title: RRJ, Pimco Invest in HK Developer PCPD’s $500 Million Bond Sale
item.description: RRJ Capital and Pacific Investment Management Co. are among investors in a $500 million bond offering from Hong Kong billionaire Richard Li’s Pacific Century Premium Developments Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: EON Set to Acquire Ovo to Create UK Energy Supply Giant
item.description: EON SE announced it would acquire Ovo Energy Ltd, potentially creating one of the largest energy providers in the UK, according to a statement.

item.title: Rolls-Royce Lines up Rare Euro Bond as It Seeks War Buffers
item.description: Rolls-Royce is planning to sell euro-denominated debt for the first time since 2020 as it seeks to buffer its business against the impact of the Middle East war.

item.title: Amazon Prepares to Issue Its First Swiss Franc Bond in AI Push
item.description: Amazon.com, Inc. is preparing to issue Swiss franc bonds for the first time, as Big Tech hyperscalers turn to new debt markets to fund artificial intelligence spending.

item.title: US Trade Delegation Likely to Visit India as Pact Talks Advance
item.description: A US trade delegation is likely to visit India for talks, a New Delhi official said Monday, as the two sides continue negotiations toward a bilateral agreement.

item.title: Reliance Is Said to Plan Offering All New Shares in Jio IPO
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. is gearing up to offer only new shares in the initial public offering of Jio Platforms Ltd., without selldowns by existing holders, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a U-turn in the preparation for what is slated to be India’s largest ever listing.

item.title: Saudi Arabian Oil Exports to China Set for Deep Plunge in June
item.description: Saudi Arabian crude oil exports to China for loading in June are set to plunge to around 13 million to 14 million barrels, according to traders informed by the producer.

item.title: India-Bound Fertilizer Shipment Scrapped Over Iran Origin Risk
item.description: An India-bound fertilizer shipment booked under an April tender has been scrapped after officials raised concerns over links to Iran, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter.
